_source: Two Homecoming forum guides, supplied by Joel: 'The Good Missions Guide — a heroic levelling journey through story arcs' (topic 13961, blueside) and 'The Mean Missions Guide — a villainous levelling journey through story arcs' (topic 21898, redside).
_credit_note: EDITORIAL, and not ours. Both guides are by Gulbasaur (the Mean guide's own text refers to 'my blueside guide', so the pair share an author), condensed here to contact chains and level bands. The forums return 403 to automated reads, so this was transcribed from Joel's paste. Credited in CREDITS.md and named on the surface wherever this layer is shown — a guide's judgement is its author's work, not a data file we parsed.
_provenance_label: The Good / Mean Missions Guides, by Gulbasaur

xp_macro.why: The guides route you through zones whose contacts have narrow level bands, and modern XP is faster than those zones were designed for. This macro makes a button that pauses and resumes XP so you don't outlevel a story mid-arc. Rule of thumb: pause at levels ending in 4 or 9.

zones.note: Free enhancements and inspirations, and a run through mission basics. Galaxy City is the weaker tutorial but connects better to the early story arcs.

Don't skip these by running Death From Below. The zone has a real story arc that foreshadows later zones, and you'll get your levels anyway. You can only do ONE of the two middle contacts; both are good and both lead to Aaron Thiery.

A hazard zone: bigger spawns, older design, but a decent story and it unlocks the Cavern of Transcendence trial. The level bands are slim — outlevelling them is the real risk. Frostfire is the mission to bring help for. If you finish Flux before Julius is available, go run Death From Below or dip into Kings Row and come back.
A short pair of neat arcs ending in a solo mission. The Shining Stars chain was cut short by the game's closure but is still worth doing — it introduces characters you meet later in tip missions.

zones.unlocks: The last mission grants the Ouroboros Portal.
zones.note: Where the modern game begins — unique map, huge variety, almost all stealthable. Also worth it in Steel Canyon: Laura Lockheart then Graham Easton (both 15-24). Before you come here, run Positron 1 & 2, Death From Below, Drowning in Blood and Cavern of Transcendence; aim to arrive around 17-18. Keep the jetpack from Positron — you'll want it in the Shadow Shard.

Praetorian storyline, and strong enough that it would be a shame to miss. The Seed of Hamidon raid boss wanders the middle of the zone — eight capable heroes take it down easily and it's worth doing for the badges.

zones.unlocks: Ernesto Hess Task Force; the Moonfire TF also starts here.
zones.note: Starts generic and builds into something much better, with a couple of very good temporary powers. Do Striga's first two contacts, cross to First Ward, then come back to finish.
Clunky at the start, memorable once it gets going, with unique maps and neat temporary powers. Giant monsters roam, two of which fight each other.

Gaslamp fantasy, world of the dead, knights and magic — unique in tone. It does just sort of stop, but characters return later. Around here: Ouroboros' The Pilgrim (25-50) then Mender Lazarus (30-39).

zones.unlocks: Progress toward the Portal Jockey accolade (permanent health and endurance).
zones.note: The centre of the old end game, and where a lot of archvillains you'll meet again get introduced. Designed as team content — this is the point to use the LFG.
Stylistically unique, time travel as a plot device, Kheldian lore. Entered through the Midnighter Mansion (Steel Canyon, Croatoa or Founders' Falls).

zones.unlocks: Lady Grey Task Force; the Rikti Mothership Raid.
zones.note: Good sense of progression and characters you've met before. The Mothership Raid is the glory lap — find it on the LFG, follow the crowd, spam AoE.

A thankless grind of repetitive missions against defence-debuffing, control-resistant enemies — and a genuine rite of passage. Enormous travel: bring mission teleporters, the Ouroboros portal and a jetpack. Do them once.
Breakout teaches door missions and how you get out of the Zig. Galaxy City puts your fall to villainy on screen if that suits your concept.

zones.content_warning: Lt Harris' last mission deals with an abusive, stalking relationship — skip it if that's a trigger. It is unusually dark for redside.
zones.note: Don't skip the zone by running Death From Below. Kallinda and Mongoose set up your status as a Destined One, the recurring redside theme; Burke and Creed foreshadow something much later.
Quiet after Mercy Island, but the few arcs are very good. Do Mr Bocor's Loa Bone mission — it's a nice temporary power. Then finish Dr Graves' next two arcs.

zones.unlocks: Marshal Brass awards Entrusted with the Secret — your Ouroboros access. The University (Dean John Yu) teaches IO crafting; Ashley McKnight (14-50) opens the Midnighter Club.
zones.note: This is where the broker system starts: newspaper missions, then a bank heist, then the contact unlocks. Stealth saves a lot of time solo. Percey's arc is the one to start with. Reuben and Hammond need no broker. Aim for level 20 so you can visit the Facemaker in Face for an extra costume slot.

Where redside comes into its own and you stop being a lackey. Macarthur needs no broker and his arc is pure Saturday-morning cartoon. Dubrovsky's chain carries the first real Captain Mako lore. Doc Buzzsaw needs the Bling badge.

contacts: Carter Mortesen (start, in Cap au Diable)
zones.note: Praetorian lore is less relevant to a villain, but the zone is too well made to skip. The Seed of Hamidon raid boss is in the middle of the zone.

A lot of lore here pays off at end game. The level bands are dense — it may be easier to unlock the 25-29 contacts, level elsewhere, and come back at 34. Mavis' chain introduces Nemesis; Shadowy Figure's is your first real Rikti thread.
Carries on from First Ward. Alternatively go to 34, do some St Martial, then come back.

contacts: Johnny Sonata (35-39, needs the Obsessed badge)
zones.unlocks: Lovely Linda (40-50) in Face opens your level-40 costume slot.
zones.note: Hardcase is the anchor here with four arcs; Johnny Sonata's set links back to them.

contacts: your Patron's four arcs (two at 40, two at 45)
zones.note: The final redside zone and home of the four Patrons, all foreshadowed by now. Ghost Widow and Scirocco carry the most lore. All four Patron chains end your Destined One storyline. Phipps shows a much darker Arachnos; Grillo closes out the Snake thread from Mercy Island.
Stylistically unique, time travel, Kheldian lore. Messy story progression, but the task force at the end is superb.

zones.unlocks: Lady Grey Task Force; the Rikti Mothership Raid.
zones.note: Good progression and returning characters. The Mothership Raid is the glory lap.
Thematically thin for a villain but it ties into Dark Astoria's lore, which is Incarnate territory. A grind, a rite of passage, and enormous travel — bring teleporters.
